title: pelma ochthochilum
intro: Pelma ochthochilum, also known as Indian gentian or Gentianella amarella, is a plant species in the gentian family that is native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent. It is believed to offer various health benefits, including reduced stress and anxiety, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of Pelma ochthochilum typically include the plant's roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and powders for medicinal purposes.
